Since `getChatChannelMeta` dynamically builds from the bundled plugin metadata, once the pulseboard package exists with proper `openclaw.plugin.json` configuration, it should be available. content.text:
All 7 files are in place. Here's a summary of what was created:

### `extensions/pulseboard/` — New PulseBoard Channel Plugin

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata (`@openclaw/pulseboard`, ESM module) with the `openclaw` block defining channel id, label, selectionLabel, docsPath, and blurb |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest with `id: "pulseboard"`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`, and a minimal `configSchema` |
| `src/types.ts` | Three interfaces: `PulseBoardAccount` (boardUrl, apiToken, username), `PulseBoardConfig` (accounts map), `PulseBoardMessage` (id, boardId, authorId, content, timestamp) |
| `src/runtime.ts` | Runtime store via `createPluginRuntimeStore` — exports `getPulseBoardRuntime` and `setPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `src/channel.ts` | Core plugin built with `createChatChannelPlugin()` — uses `getChatChannelMeta`, defines capabilities (direct + group, no media), config/setup/gateway/messaging stubs, and outbound with `deliveryMode: "direct"`, `textChunkLimit: 4000`, and a `sendText` stub using `attachChannelToResult` |
| `index.ts` | Plugin entry via `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with id, name, description, plugin, and setRuntime — follows the IRC pattern exactly |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup entry via `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` — matches the IRC/Nostr pattern |

The plugin follows the established patterns from `extensions/irc/` and `extensions/nostr/`, importing only from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths (respecting the extension boundary rules), and no existing files were modified.

result:
All 7 files are in place. Here's a summary of what was created:

### `extensions/pulseboard/` — New PulseBoard Channel Plugin

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package metadata (`@openclaw/pulseboard`, ESM module) with the `openclaw` block defining channel id, label, selectionLabel, docsPath, and blurb |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest with `id: "pulseboard"`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`, and a minimal `configSchema` |
| `src/types.ts` | Three interfaces: `PulseBoardAccount` (boardUrl, apiToken, username), `PulseBoardConfig` (accounts map), `PulseBoardMessage` (id, boardId, authorId, content, timestamp) |
| `src/runtime.ts` | Runtime store via `createPluginRuntimeStore` — exports `getPulseBoardRuntime` and `setPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `src/channel.ts` | Core plugin built with `createChatChannelPlugin()` — uses `getChatChannelMeta`, defines capabilities (direct + group, no media), config/setup/gateway/messaging stubs, and outbound with `deliveryMode: "direct"`, `textChunkLimit: 4000`, and a `sendText` stub using `attachChannelToResult` |
| `index.ts` | Plugin entry via `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with id, name, description, plugin, and setRuntime — follows the IRC pattern exactly |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup entry via `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` — matches the IRC/Nostr pattern |

The plugin follows the established patterns from `extensions/irc/` and `extensions/nostr/`, importing only from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` subpaths (respecting the extension boundary rules), and no existing files were modified.
